title: UDLM Resource Type Specification
description: Meta-schema that every Resource Type Specification in the UDLM Resource Type Registry MUST validate against. JSON Schema 2020-12 is the normative wire format for entity-type definitions (contracts/schema-sharing.md). A Resource Type Specification is the versioned, formal definition of a Resource Type: field schema, constraints, lifecycle rules, portability classification, typed realized outputs, and allowed relationship types (entities/resource-type-hierarchy.md). This meta-schema is itself part of the UDLM spec and is versioned on the SPEC axis.

$id.description: Canonical, project-controlled identifier on udlm.dev (HTTPS-enforced via the .dev HSTS preload). Encodes BOTH version axes and mirrors `conformsTo`: https://udlm.dev/registry/udlm/<spec>/<TypeName>/<version>. TypeName is `Category.Type` for the Resource family (Compute.VirtualMachine) or single-segment for the Knowledge family (Capability). Relationships reference a target by its `$id`. A new MAJOR mints a new `$id`.

conformsTo.description: SPEC-axis binding: the UDLM spec major.minor this type is written against (its apiVersion). Peers conformant to the same MAJOR are wire-compatible (CONFORMANCE.md §9).

uuid.description: RFC 4122 UUIDv4 identity of this type specification (contracts/identifier-scheme.md). Immutable for the life of the type.

resourceType.description: Type name, unique within its `family`. Resource family: namespaced Category.Type (Compute.VirtualMachine, Network.IPAddress). Knowledge family: single-segment (Capability, TaxonomyTerm) — disambiguated by `family` (entities/knowledge-family.md).

version.description: ENTITY-axis version: this type's own Major.Minor.Revision (see registry/VERSIONING.md). Immutable once published; any change produces a new version.

family.description: Entity-type family (foundations/entity-type-families.md) — a logical grouping for discovery, NOT a usage boundary.

entityType.description: The entity type within its family. Resource: Infrastructure Resource | Composite | Process. Knowledge: Capability | TaxonomyTerm | Alias | Antipattern | UseCase. Constrained by `family` (see allOf). (foundations/entity-types.md, entities/knowledge-family.md.)

lifecycleArchetype.description: How this type interprets the universal four states: `provisioning` (Resource — Intent→Requested→Realized→Discovered via a provider) or `curation` (Knowledge — proposed→under-review→canonical→deprecated). Optional; inferred from `family` if absent.

portability.description: Portability classification (entities/resource-type-hierarchy.md). Any deviation from full portability MUST be explicitly declared.

status.description: Lifecycle status of this version (foundations/layering-and-versioning.md universal deprecation model).

deprecation.description: REQUIRED when status is deprecated or retired. A breaking (MAJOR) successor MUST supply migrationGuidance.

spec.description: The field schema for the Intent/Requested state — the wire contract every provider offering this type MUST satisfy. A nested JSON Schema 2020-12 fragment. Catalog items / constraint profiles (E1) project over these fields by path; they never widen them.

immutable.description: Dot-paths into `spec` that are createOnly — set at Intent, never changed post-realization; a change forces replace → supersession. Use for sovereignty-determining fields (zone / classification). Declarative noun; DCM enforces it (R3 / cross-cutting P4).

outputs.description: Typed Realized-state outputs (E2): named values this type publishes when it reaches Realized. Other entities bind to these via dependency references; the binding is contract-checked against these types rather than string-spliced.

relationships.description: Allowed relationship types from this resource to others (entities/entity-relationships.md, service-dependencies.md).

targetField.description: An `outputs` key on the target this edge binds to (e.g. a database's `connectionString`). The binding is a typed reference resolved at dispatch — data movement, not transformation (T4).

lifecycle.description: Lifecycle rules. Defaults to UDLM's four-state model Intent → Requested → Realized → Discovered (foundations/four-states.md). Override only to declare type-specific decommission behavior.

adopts.description: External standards this type ADOPTS by reference (core-tenets T5, design-principles/adopted-standards.md). The standard's schema is NOT restated here — only an identity join + a version-pinned conformance pointer. Negotiating/translating versions is Policy (DCM), never the data model.

aliases.description: Alternative names this type is known by (AKA / cross-walk for compatibility), for names NOT already covered by an `adopts[].standardName`. Use for vendor/colloquial names that do not correspond to a formally-adopted standard (e.g. AWS 'Dedicated Host', OpenStack 'Ironic node'). Names from standards this type formally adopts belong on `adopts[].standardName` instead.

standard.description: OPTIONAL: the standard/vendor/ecosystem this name comes from, e.g. AWS, OpenStack.

standardVersion.description: A reference over an external standard's OWN versioning scheme — a pin ("1.4"), a wildcard ("1.x"), or a range expression (">=1.2 <2.0"). The schema only checks it is a non-empty string; RANGE RESOLUTION / negotiation is Policy (DCM), not the data model (T2/T5).

adoptedStandardRef.description: A reference to an external standard this entity adopts (T5; design-principles/adopted-standards.md §3.1). Carries provenance + version pointer + recorded license verdict — never the standard's schema. `identityJoin` is present only for data-join adoptions (e.g. FOCUS cost records keyed by ResourceId); reference-vocabulary adoptions (Redfish/Metal3/DNS field names) omit it. Provenance (`source`) and the license verdict (`license` + `licenseCompatibility`) are MANDATORY (SPEC-DESIGN-REQUIREMENTS §22–23).

standard.description: External standard name, e.g. FOCUS, OpenCost, Redfish, Metal3, OSCAL, SCIM, OpenTelemetry.

standardName.description: OPTIONAL cross-walk: the name THIS standard uses for this type/concept (e.g. Redfish 'ComputerSystem', Metal3 'BareMetalHost'). Together with `standard` + `version` this is the {alternative name, spec, spec version} translation for compatibility — a consumer using the standard's vocabulary can resolve its term to this UDLM type.

role.description: What the adopted data/vocabulary is for (e.g. 'cost-and-usage data for this resource').

identityJoin.description: OPTIONAL. The anchor only UDLM can provide for a DATA-join adoption: a local identity field bound to a column in the standard's records (e.g. uuid -> FOCUS ResourceId). Omit for reference-vocabulary adoptions that borrow field names without joining to the standard's records.

localField.description: Field on this entity used as the join key (e.g. uuid).

standardColumn.description: Column/attribute in the standard's records that the local field joins to (e.g. ResourceId).

source.description: Canonical URL of the standard (e.g. https://focus.finops.org/). REQUIRED provenance (SPEC-DESIGN-REQUIREMENTS §22).

license.description: The source's license identifier (SPDX where possible, e.g. CC-BY-4.0, Apache-2.0, GPL-3.0). REQUIRED (SPEC-DESIGN-REQUIREMENTS §23).

licenseCompatibility.description: Recorded verdict of the license check against UDLM's Apache-2.0 (SPEC-DESIGN-REQUIREMENTS §23): `compatible-reference` = we only reference the vocabulary (field names = facts), license-clean regardless of source license (the default); `compatible-vendor` = source is Apache-2.0-compatible, so its text MAY be absorbed/vendored; `reference-only` = copyleft/file-scoped source (GPL/LGPL/MPL) whose names MAY be referenced but whose text/files MUST NOT be vendored.
